title: medicago italica subsp. corrugata
intro: Medicago italica subsp. corrugata, also known as Italian lucerne or Italian clover, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its distinctive corrugated leaves and ability to fix nitrogen in the soil. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Medicago italica subsp. corrugata include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as providing relief from symptoms of menopause and other hormonal imbalances. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as an infusion or tincture, and its leaves can be added to salads or used as a cooking green, or made into a tea, to take advantage of its medicinal and nutritional properties.
